purpose: A multi-level code analysis and processing system that orchestrates agents to harvest, detect, divide, annotate, and validate code structures with LLM integration.

modules.description: Command-line interface with commands for context retrieval and code mapping, including display utilities and UI constants.
Configuration management including environment variable parsing, default settings, and configuration models.
Orchestration layer managing pipeline execution, checkpoints, deltas, graph repair, versioning, and progress tracking.
Core utilities including error handling, validation, metrics logging, git operations, LLM client, concurrency management, and type definitions.
Multi-level processing pipeline with Level 0 (harvesting), Level 1 (detection), Level 2 (dividing), Level 3 (annotation), and Level 4 (validation/autofix).
Query engine for filtering, ranking, formatting, and validating code analysis results.
Comprehensive test suite covering all modules with unit and integration tests.
Audit reports and refactoring plans documenting code analysis, improvements, and architectural decisions.
Documentation including architecture, CLI usage, configuration, and validation guides.

conventions: Uses barrel exports (index.ts) for module organization across all directories
Test files colocated in separate tests/ directory mirroring src/ structure
Multi-level processing pipeline architecture (Level 0-4) with each level having dedicated modules
Comprehensive audit and documentation in separate directories
TypeScript strict mode with tsconfig.json configuration
Markdown documentation for architecture, CLI, configuration, and validation
Separation of concerns: CLI, config, coordinator, core utilities, processing levels, and query engine
Each level contains parsers, validators, and prompt templates as needed
